decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. Previous attempts to maximize spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500 W/mK), and geometry changes are constrained by legality limits. The most effective remaining lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction from the spreader to the side sinks. We will increase base_material conductivity by 1.5x (from 12.0 to 18.0), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and bounds [5.0, 250.0].

changes.reason: Increasing base plate conductivity reduces thermal resistance between the heat spreader and the cooled boundaries, facilitating better lateral heat removal to lower the chip peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved heat flux distribution through the base layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts entirely to the spreader or interface resistance
Further increases may be needed if a single step does not close the 4.26°C gap
